docs.text: A Framework for data handling. It provides an adapter for beans and maps with JavaFX Property support.
docs.title: Sapphire Data

docs.text:
Sapphire Data is heavily used in the sapphire ui frameworks:
sapphire-core sapphire-extension
docs.title: Sapphire Frameworks

docs.text: Build and tested against Scala 2.12/2.13 and JDK 11 - 14
docs.title: Cross Build

docs.text:
Java UI Application Framework as replacement for Swing.
Sapphire depends on OpenJFX base package for properties handling.
https://openjfx.io
docs.title: JavaFX

docs.text: Expressions are resolved by EL 3 Tomcat Expression Language.
docs.title: Expression Language

docs.text: Sapphire is published to Bintray and linked to Maven Central.
docs.title: Maven

docs.text: resolvers += "sfxcode-bintray" at "https://dl.bintray.com/sfxcode/maven"
docs.title: Repository

docs.text:
sbt libraryDependencies += "com.sfxcode.sapphire" % "sapphire-data_2.12" % "1.0.2-SNAPSHOT" Maven <dependency>
  <groupId>com.sfxcode.sapphire</groupId>
  <artifactId>sapphire-data_2.12</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.2-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency> Gradle dependencies {
  compile group: 'com.sfxcode.sapphire', name: 'sapphire-data_2.12', version: '1.0.2-SNAPSHOT'
}
docs.title: Artifact

docs.text: Apache 2
docs.title: Licence

docs.text: DataAdapter is one of the core concepts of this framework. It is an adapter for Java/Scala Beans (Maps) with automatic JavaFX Binding.
docs.title: Adapter

docs.text: Every Java / Scala Bean can be used for DataAdapter Bean member value lookup by java reflection DataAdapter has additional support for Java / Scala Maps DataAdapter can resolve Expressions on bean DataAdapter creates Properties needed for Binding on demand DataAdapter has change management by default DataAdapter implements java.util.Interface => Scala classes and maps can be used like java maps
docs.title: Features
Example

docs.text: Expression Language is based on Tomcat Expression Language .
docs.title: Expression Language

docs.text: EL supports defining own functions in expressions. There is a FunctionHelper in the Expressions object. Some Functions are predefined.
docs.title: Functions

docs.text: Expression Language Processing is defined in the Expressions object.
docs.title: Base Usage

docs.text:
For Report Generation JasperReports can be used. It is based on the itext library.
For PDF reports the PdfExporter can be used. As DataSource implementation is a AdapterDataSource available.
As visual report editor you can use Jaspersoft Studio (Community Edition).
docs.title: Reports

docs.text:
DataAdapter is used for building a rewindable JasperReports DataSource.
In the Templates (.jrxml) items can be accessed like maps.
docs.title: AdapterDataSource

docs.text:
PDFExporter simplifies the export to PDF task. You need only a JasperReports File (compiled or uncompiled).
Report Parameter are optional by creation a Map of key/values. DataSource is optional (use a AdapterDataSource if needed) optional exporter / report configuration
docs.title: PDFExporter

docs.text:
Because of Field validation in reports a workaround is needed for Expression values.
To use a Field with:
${_self.getValue('name')}
you have to replace Expression start with # and expression end with @ .
#_self.getValue('name')@
docs.title: Expressions in Fields

docs.text:
# Changes

## Versions

### 1.0.1
* added optional report support (jasperreports)

### 1.0.0
* Data Functions extracted from sapphire-core
* DataAdapter with java.util.Map Support
